003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.filter;
018
019import static org.apache.juneau.commons.utils.AssertionUtils.*;
020
021import java.util.*;
022import java.util.function.*;
023import java.util.regex.*;
024
025import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
026
027import jakarta.servlet.http.*;
028
029/**
030 * Per-request id mint-or-honor filter that stashes the id on the servlet request and echoes it on the response.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * Designed to be invoked from a {@link RestStartCall @RestStartCall} method so the id is available to every
034 * downstream component — the call logger, observability layers, and any application code that reads
035 * {@code req.getAttribute(RestServerConstants.REQUEST_ID)}.
036 *
037 * <h5 class='topic'>Behavior</h5>
038 *
039 * <ul>
040 *    <li>If the incoming request carries an {@code X-Request-Id} header and the value matches the configured
041 *       {@linkplain Builder#validator(Predicate) validator}, that value is honored.
042 *    <li>Otherwise (header absent, blank, or rejected by the validator), a fresh id is minted via the configured
043 *       {@linkplain Builder#idSupplier(Supplier) supplier} (default: {@link UUID#randomUUID()}).
044 *    <li>The chosen id is stashed on the underlying servlet request under the
045 *       {@link RestServerConstants#REQUEST_ID REQUEST_ID} attribute key.
046 *    <li>The chosen id is echoed on the response as {@code X-Request-Id}.
047 * </ul>
048 *
049 * <h5 class='topic'>Default validator</h5>
050 *
051 * <p>
052 * The default validator is {@code ^[A-Za-z0-9-_]{1,128}$}, which accepts UUIDs and the bulk of distributed-tracing
053 * id schemes (W3C Trace Context, OpenTelemetry, Datadog, etc.) while rejecting whitespace, control characters,
054 * header-injection payloads, and oversized strings.  Customize via {@link Builder#validator(Predicate)}.
055 *
056 * <h5 class='topic'>Example usage</h5>
057 *
058 * <p class='bjava'>
059 *    <ja>@Rest</ja>
060 *    <jk>public class</jk> ApiResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
061 *
062 *       <jk>private static final</jk> RequestIdFilter <jsf>REQUEST_ID</jsf> = RequestIdFilter.<jsm>create</jsm>().build();
063 *
064 *       <ja>@RestStartCall</ja>
065 *       <jk>public void</jk> stampRequestId(HttpServletRequest <jv>req</jv>, HttpServletResponse <jv>res</jv>) {
066 *          <jsf>REQUEST_ID</jsf>.apply(<jv>req</jv>, <jv>res</jv>);
067 *       }
068 *    }
069 * </p>
070 *
071 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
072 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerRateLimitAndRequestId">REST Server — Rate-Limiting and Request-Id Propagation</a>
073 * </ul>
074 *
075 * @since 10.0.0
076 */
077@SuppressWarnings({
078   "java:S1192" // Duplicate string literals are HTTP header names (e.g. X-Request-ID); intentional
079})
080public class RequestIdFilter {
081
082   /** Standard request and response header name for the request id. */
083   public static final String HEADER_REQUEST_ID = "X-Request-Id";
084
085   /** Default validator pattern.  Matches UUIDs and most distributed-tracing id schemes. */
086   public static final String DEFAULT_VALIDATOR_PATTERN = "^[A-Za-z0-9-_]{1,128}$";
087
088   private final Supplier<String> idSupplier;
089   private final Predicate<String> validator;
090   private final String attributeKey;
091
092   /**
093    * Constructor.
094    *
095    * @param b The builder configuring this filter.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
096    */
097   protected RequestIdFilter(Builder b) {
098      assertArgNotNull("builder", b);
099      this.idSupplier = b.idSupplier != null ? b.idSupplier : () -> UUID.randomUUID().toString();
100      this.validator = b.validator != null ? b.validator : Pattern.compile(DEFAULT_VALIDATOR_PATTERN).asPredicate();
101      this.attributeKey = b.attributeKey;
102   }
103
104   /**
105    * Creates a new builder.
106    *
107    * @return A new builder.
108    */
109   public static Builder create() {
110      return new Builder();
111   }
112
113   /**
114    * Mints or honors a request id, stashes it on the request, and echoes it on the response.
115    *
116    * <p>
117    * Idempotent: if the attribute is already set on the servlet request (for example by a parent filter), the
118    * existing value is honored and re-echoed.
119    *
120    * @param req The servlet request.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
121    * @param res The servlet response.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
122    * @return The resolved request id.  Never <jk>null</jk>.
123    */
124   public String apply(HttpServletRequest req, HttpServletResponse res) {
125      assertArgNotNull("req", req);
126      assertArgNotNull("res", res);
127      var existing = req.getAttribute(attributeKey);
128      if (existing instanceof String s && ! s.isEmpty()) {
129         res.setHeader(HEADER_REQUEST_ID, s);
130         return s;
131      }
132      var incoming = req.getHeader(HEADER_REQUEST_ID);
133      var id = (incoming != null && validator.test(incoming)) ? incoming : idSupplier.get();
134      req.setAttribute(attributeKey, id);
135      res.setHeader(HEADER_REQUEST_ID, id);
136      return id;
137   }
138
139   /**
140    * Builder for {@link RequestIdFilter}.
141    */
142   public static class Builder {
143
144      Supplier<String> idSupplier;
145      Predicate<String> validator;
146      String attributeKey = RestServerConstants.REQUEST_ID;
147
148      /**
149       * Constructor.
150       */
151      protected Builder() {}
152
153      /**
154       * Sets the supplier used to mint a new id when none is honored from the request.
155       *
156       * <p>
157       * Default is {@link UUID#randomUUID()}.  Swap in a smaller / shorter id scheme (e.g. a 16-byte
158       * base32 token) when payload size matters.
159       *
160       * @param value The supplier.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
161       * @return This object.
162       */
163      public Builder idSupplier(Supplier<String> value) {
164         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
165         idSupplier = value;
166         return this;
167      }
168
169      /**
170       * Sets the predicate used to validate an incoming {@code X-Request-Id} header.
171       *
172       * <p>
173       * Default is {@code Pattern.compile("^[A-Za-z0-9-_]{1,128}$").asPredicate()} — accepts UUIDs and most
174       * distributed-tracing id schemes while rejecting whitespace, control characters, and oversize values.
175       * Values that fail validation are discarded and a fresh id is minted.
176       *
177       * @param value The predicate.  Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
178       * @return This object.
179       */
180      public Builder validator(Predicate<String> value) {
181         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
182         validator = value;
183         return this;
184      }
185
186      /**
187       * Overrides the servlet-request attribute key under which the id is stashed.
188       *
189       * <p>
190       * Defaults to {@link RestServerConstants#REQUEST_ID}.  Override only when
191       * coexisting with a third-party filter that publishes the id under a different key.
192       *
193       * @param value The attribute key.  Must not be <jk>null</jk> or blank.
194       * @return This object.
195       */
196      public Builder attributeKey(String value) {
197         assertArgNotNull("value", value);
198         if (value.isBlank())
199            throw new IllegalArgumentException("Argument 'value' must not be blank.");
200         attributeKey = value;
201         return this;
202      }
203
204      /**
205       * Builds the filter.
206       *
207       * @return A new {@link RequestIdFilter}.
208       */
209      public RequestIdFilter build() {
210         return new RequestIdFilter(this);
211      }
212   }
213}
